title: calceolaria glandulosa subsp. glandulosa
intro: Calceolaria glandulosa subsp. glandulosa, also known as the Slipper Flower or Pocket Flower, is a flowering plant species native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Calceolaria glandulosa subsp. glandulosa include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional Andean medicine to treat various health conditions.
